$comment: ADVERSARIAL: receipt rows committing to forbidden fields per README §'MUST NOT commit (security-load-bearing)'. A conformant implementation MUST refuse to emit a receipt that contains any of the field names in FORBIDDEN_RECEIPT_FIELDS — in any form: raw, hashed, partial, length, base64'd, hex'd. The rejection happens at the receipt-build layer, BEFORE the signature is computed, so a leaky receipt never reaches the signing key.

vectors.name: leak_credential_value_raw
vectors.description: Most obvious leak — receipt row contains the credential as 'credential_value'. The MUST-NOT-COMMIT list in README treats this as the canary case; any impl that fails this fails the entire spec.

vectors.name: leak_credential_partial
vectors.description: 'Just the first 4 chars / last 4 chars' is still a leak — narrows credential brute-force search space by ~24 bits. The spec covers partial leakage explicitly: 'in any form: raw, hashed, partial, length'.

vectors.name: leak_credential_length
vectors.description: Even credential LENGTH is forbidden. Length distinguishes service types (OpenAI ~51 chars, Anthropic ~108 chars, GitHub PAT ~40 chars) — a third-party observing length leakage can infer which credential type was used per call, breaking the audit-blind-to-service property.

vectors.name: leak_allowed_subs
vectors.description: Receipts MUST NOT enumerate who else is allowed to use a credential. A receipt that committed to allowedSubs would turn the audit chain into a 'list every peer authorized for this service' oracle — a separate enumeration vector.

vectors.name: leak_request_body
vectors.description: Request body MUST NOT be in the receipt — the body may contain user PII (chat content, document text, search queries). The receipt commits to request SIZE only.
